exercises.name: barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Max strength, overload potential, mass building.

exercises.name: incline dumbbell press
exercises.notes: Upper chest development, shoulder balance, unilateral control.

exercises.name: dip
exercises.notes: Lean forward to bias chest; vertical torso biases triceps.

exercises.name: cable fly
exercises.notes: Constant tension; angle sets upper vs lower bias.

exercises.name: chin-up
exercises.notes: Supinated grip; often more biceps than pull-up.

exercises.name: deadlift
exercises.notes: Total-body strength, hinge power, posterior chain.

exercises.name: straight-arm pulldown
exercises.notes: Isolation, lat activation in lengthened range.

exercises.name: leg press
exercises.notes: High volume safely; stance shifts quad vs glute bias.

exercises.name: leg curl
exercises.notes: Knee-flexion role of hamstrings; prehab support.

exercises.name: standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Gastroc emphasis; jumping and ankle strength.

guide_sections.title: Overview
guide_sections.content: This knowledge base maps major muscle groups to regions, example exercises, targets, and benefits. Use it for exercise selection, balance, and progression. Progressive overload drives adaptation. Use full ROM where pain-free. Balance push/pull and quad/hinge patterns.

guide_sections.title: Chest (Pectorals)
guide_sections.content: Regions: upper chest (clavicular head), mid chest (sternal), lower chest (costal). Barbell bench press targets mid chest, front delts, triceps — max strength and mass. Incline dumbbell press biases upper chest. Decline press and forward-leaning dips emphasize lower chest. Cable fly offers constant tension and angle control for hypertrophy and mind-muscle connection.

guide_sections.title: Back
guide_sections.content: Regions: lats (width), mid traps/rhomboids (thickness), lower traps, spinal erectors, rear delts/teres. Pull-up/chin-up build lats, upper back, and grip. Barbell row hits mid back and lats. Seated cable row suits controlled hypertrophy and retraction. Deadlift trains the whole posterior chain. Straight-arm pulldown isolates lats in a lengthened position.

guide_sections.title: Shoulders
guide_sections.content: Regions: front, side, and rear delts. Overhead press builds pressing strength and mass. Lateral raises target side delts for width. Rear delt fly supports posture and balance. Arnold press adds ROM and hypertrophy stimulus.

guide_sections.title: Arms — Biceps and Triceps
guide_sections.content: Biceps: long head (outer peak), short head (inner thickness), brachialis. Barbell curl for overall size; incline curl for long head stretch; preacher for short-head isolation; hammer for brachialis and forearms. Triceps: long, lateral, medial heads. Close-grip bench for overload; overhead extension for long head; pushdowns for lateral/medial isolation; skullcrushers for overall triceps mass and lockout.

guide_sections.title: Legs
guide_sections.content: Quads: rectus femoris and vastii — back squat and front squat for strength; leg press for volume; leg extension for isolation. Hamstrings: RDL and leg curl for hinge and knee-flexion roles. Glutes: hip thrust, Bulgarian split squat, walking lunge. Calves: standing vs seated raises for gastroc vs soleus.

guide_sections.title: Calves
guide_sections.content: Standing calf raises (knee straight) emphasize gastrocnemius. Seated calf raises (knee bent) bias soleus. For balance, consider light tibialis anterior work (e.g., tib raises) if needed.

guide_sections.title: Core and trunk
guide_sections.content: Regions include rectus abdominis, obliques, transverse abdominis, and lumbar stabilizers. Plank trains bracing; hanging leg raises challenge flexion control; cable crunch loads the rectus; Russian twists hit rotation; back extensions support erectors and glutes.

guide_sections.title: Forearms and grip
guide_sections.content: Farmer carries build grip, traps, and core. Wrist curls and reverse wrist curls train flexors and extensors for elbow resilience and grip support.

guide_sections.title: Programming
guide_sections.content: Strength focus: about 3–6 reps, longer rests. Hypertrophy: about 6–15 reps, moderate rests. Endurance: 15+ reps. Progressive overload drives adaptation. Use full ROM where pain-free. Balance push/pull and squat/hinge patterns.

guide_sections.title: Goals — quick reference
guide_sections.content: Build size: bench, rows, squats, presses, curls, pushdowns, leg press. Build strength: squat, deadlift, bench, overhead press, pull-up. Improve posture: rows, rear delt fly, face pull, planks, RDL. Fat loss: prioritize compounds, walking, and nutrition adherence.
